s: The grammar pattern たら means 'if, when (verb form)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'たら' mean?
d: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
e: Pattern: Verb past た-form+ら. When I wake up

s: The grammar pattern 〜ね means 'isn't it?'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜ね' mean?
e: Pattern: Sentence+ね. Isn't this nice!

s: The grammar pattern ないように means 'to avoid'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ないように' mean?
e: to avoid; to prevent

s: The grammar pattern 〜だ means 'The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜だ' mean?
a: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: Pattern: Sentence+だ. I'm English.

s: The grammar pattern 少し means 'a bit...'
q: What does the grammar pattern '少し' mean?
e: A bit cold

s: The grammar pattern すぎる means 'too; too much; excessively'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'すぎる' mean?
d: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
e: Pattern: Verb stem+すぎる, な-adjective (without な)+すぎる, い-adjective (without い)+すぎる. This problem is too difficult for me

s: The grammar pattern たり する　form means 'doing things like V+ing'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'たり する　form' mean?
e: Yesterday I did things like watching tv and playing video games.

s: The grammar pattern そう means 'Looks like'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'そう' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb ます-stem+そう+(だ), い-adjective without い+そう+(だ), な-adjective (without な)+そう+(だ), Negative form ~ない without い+さ+そう+(だ). Looks like snow will fall

s: The grammar pattern がる means 'to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'がる' mean?
a: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
d: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
e: Pattern: い adjective (without い)+がる, な adjective+がる. He (looks like) he wants a new car.

s: The grammar pattern 〜始める means 'to begin/start VERB-ing'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜始める' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb stem+始める. I started reading yesterday.

s: The grammar pattern 〜は means ''topic marker' (also shown as 'wa')'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜は' mean?
d: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
e: X wa Y desu : X is Y

s: The grammar pattern なので means 'since'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'なので' mean?
e: since this is secret

s: The grammar pattern 〜など means 'and etc.'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜など' mean?
e: and so on

s: The grammar pattern 〜も means '(with -ve) neither ... nor , also not, either'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜も' mean?
d: it is permitted and allowed to do so
someone is supposed to act in this way
to attempt something again and again
however and yet despite all this
in the not too distant future
you are required to complete this task
then and only then will it occur
to engage in an activity multiple times
e: That isn't mine either.

s: The grammar pattern どうして means 'why?, what for?'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'どうして' mean?
e: Why didn't you come?

s: The grammar pattern けれども means 'but, however'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'けれども' mean?
e: It rained, but everybody enjoyed themselves.

s: The grammar pattern でも means 'but'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'でも' mean?
e: I'd like to make it easier to use but still not use frames.

s: The grammar pattern 〜か means '? (basic question particle)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜か' mean?
e: Pattern: Sentence+か. Is this your book?

s: The grammar pattern 〜へ means 'indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e''
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜へ' mean?
a: indicates direction of verb. Also pronounced 'e'
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
e: I will go abroad next year.

s: The grammar pattern ので means 'because, and'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ので' mean?
d: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.
e: Pattern: Verb (casual)+ので, い-adjective+ので, な-adjective+な+ので, Noun+な+ので.

s: The grammar pattern ないで means 'without'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ないで' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb nai form+で. "I went to school without eating breakfast."

s: The grammar pattern より means 'more ____'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'より' mean?
e: Pattern: より+Something. Don't you know a better method?

s: The grammar pattern つもり (積もり・積り) means 'One's intention; conviction; belief'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'つもり (積もり・積り)' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+つもり+(だ), Verb nai form+つもり+(だ), Verb dictionary form+つもり+だった, Verb nai form+つもり+だった. I intend to do that; I am convinced that ...

s: The grammar pattern 欲しい means 'to want something (desirative)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '欲しい' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb て-form+ほしい, Noun+が+ほしい. I want a car.

s: The grammar pattern 頂きます means 'to receive'
q: What does the grammar pattern '頂きます' mean?
d: The 'copula' : expresses conditions. X is Y.

s: The grammar pattern から〜まで means 'from ... until'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'から〜まで' mean?
e: from spring until autumn

s: The grammar pattern しかし (然し・併し) means 'However, but'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'しかし (然し・併し)' mean?
e: But now things have changed a lot.

s: The grammar pattern 以上 means 'exceeding, above'
q: What does the grammar pattern '以上' mean?
e: Pattern: Noun (quantity)+以上. More than two hours

s: The grammar pattern 〜が means 'but, however, still'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜が' mean?
e: Pattern: Sentence 1+が+Sentence 2. I know him but we've never met.

s: The grammar pattern 〜に means '(location marker, time marker, direction marker)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜に' mean?
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: Pattern: Noun+に. There's a dog in that car.

s: The grammar pattern いくら (幾等) means 'how much'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'いくら (幾等)' mean?
e: How much is a postcard?

s: The grammar pattern 〜で means 'by means of, by, [action] at a place'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜で' mean?
a: by means of, by, [action] at a place
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: I came by car.

s: The grammar pattern 〜が means '(subject marker)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜が' mean?
e: Pattern: Subject (noun)+が+Verb. There is a television in the kitchen.

s: The grammar pattern 何 means 'What'
q: What does the grammar pattern '何' mean?
e: What will you have?

s: The grammar pattern まで means 'till, to'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'まで' mean?
e: Opening hours are 9am to 5pm.

s: The grammar pattern 〜を means ''object marker' (also shown as 'o') 目的'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜を' mean?
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
e: Pattern: Noun+を+Verb. That [←marked by object marker] please.

s: The grammar pattern 〜も means 'also'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜も' mean?
e: This is also mine

s: The grammar pattern てから means 'after'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'てから' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb て-Form+から, Noun+から, 動詞（て形）+から, 名詞+から. From 9 to 5.

s: The grammar pattern 〜に means 'to, from (indirect object marker)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜に' mean?
d: consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
to attempt something again and again
after a long time has passed
e: I was given this by my mother.

s: The grammar pattern 〜の means ''s, of'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜の' mean?
e: Pattern: Noun+の. Fred's car. / Dixon of Dock Green

s: The grammar pattern から means 'because'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'から' mean?
e: Pattern: sentence one+から+sentence two.

s: The grammar pattern 〜たい means 'I want to (verb)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜たい' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb stem+たい. netai: I want to sleep

s: The grammar pattern これ(此・是・之・惟)、それ(其), あれ(彼) means 'this, that, the other'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'これ(此・是・之・惟)、それ(其), あれ(彼)' mean?
d: 1) just, only, simply, merely; 2) as much as X wants/like/can (whatever, anything)
e: this is mine, that is yours and the other is anybody's guess.

s: The grammar pattern だけ means '1) just, only, simply, merely; 2) as much as X wants/like/can (whatever, anything)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'だけ' mean?
a: 1) just, only, simply, merely; 2) as much as X wants/like/can (whatever, anything)
e: 1) don't just love her; 2) eat as much as you want (eat whatever/anything you'd like)
